stories.title: Pentagon confirms nearly 100 troops injured in Iran war since July 7
stories.description: The death toll has risen to 17 since Operation Epic Fury began in February, with three service members killed over the weekend.

stories.title: Israel says Iran moved centrifuges into Pickaxe Mountain
stories.description: The intelligence was shared with the U.S. as Trump weighs strikes on the fortified underground nuclear site near Natanz.

stories.title: Congo Ebola death toll hits 930 as outbreak accelerates
stories.description: WHO calls it the third-largest Ebola outbreak on record, now expanding faster than any previous one.

stories.title: Ex-soldier arrested after fire outside NYC Federal Plaza
stories.description: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, allegedly poured gasoline and ignited fireworks outside 26 Federal Plaza in an anti-ICE attack that injured three people.

stories.title: New UK PM Burnham speaks with Trump on defense, Hormuz
stories.description: Hours after taking office Monday, Burnham congratulated Trump on the World Cup and pledged UK commitment to securing the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Judge freezes Paramount's $111B Warner Bros. merger
stories.description: A federal judge granted 12 states a temporary restraining order blocking the deal, which Paramount had planned to close this week.

stories.title: Pentagon denies hiding troop injuries after undisclosed Iran strikes revealed
stories.description: The Defense Department pushed back on a New York Times report that three earlier Iranian strikes on U.S. forces in Jordan went unreported.

stories.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian autos, dairy, alcohol
stories.description: The duties, invoked under Section 338, take effect in 30 days and would apply even to goods previously protected under USMCA.

stories.title: Ortega declares Nicaragua will never hold elections again
stories.description: The 80-year-old president made the announcement during a speech marking the 47th anniversary of the Sandinista revolution on Sunday.

stories.title: Pakistan, Qatar urge US and Iran to accept two-week ceasefire
stories.description: The joint proposal calls for a return to pre-July 9 positions, reopening the Strait of Hormuz, and lifting the US blockade to restart stalled talks.

stories.title: Sen. Darline Graham launches bid for full Senate term
stories.description: Graham told Fox News' Sean Hannity she's "in" the Aug. 11 special GOP primary, backed by Trump's endorsement, to succeed her late brother Lindsey.

stories.title: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder El Mayo Zambada sentenced to life in U.S. prison
stories.description: The 76-year-old drug kingpin also faces $15 billion in financial penalties, though experts say recovering more than a fraction is unlikely.

stories.title: Houthis declare naval blockade against Saudi Arabia
stories.description: The militant group announced an immediate maritime embargo, framing it as retaliation for nearly 12 years of Saudi siege on Yemen's ports.

stories.title: Qatar-gifted Air Force One to get security upgrades this fall, White House says
stories.description: Trump will revert to the older presidential aircraft while the Qatari-donated jet undergoes about a month of enhancements to its defensive capabilities.

stories.title: Hamas elects chief negotiator al-Hayya as new leader
stories.description: The group's top ceasefire negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in October 2024, after a months-long internal vote.

stories.title: Rubio says US open to diplomacy after ninth night of Iran strikes
stories.description: The secretary of state confirmed three service member deaths and called on allies to help defend the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: DOJ sought phone records of NYT journalists' relatives
stories.description: The Trump administration subpoenaed phone records of reporters' family members, including one journalist's mother, in its Air Force One leak probe.

stories.title: Ukraine fires 400 drones at Moscow region overnight
stories.description: The massive drone barrage followed Russia's record ballistic missile attack on Kyiv the previous night, which killed at least one person and wounded 16.

stories.title: Andy Burnham takes office as UK prime minister
stories.description: The former Greater Manchester mayor succeeds Keir Starmer, pledging to "rewire" Britain with a focus on devolution and cost-of-living relief.

stories.title: Trump vows Netanyahu won't be arrested in the U.S.
stories.description: The president responded on Truth Social to NYC Mayor Mamdani's push to detain the Israeli prime minister during September's UN General Assembly.

stories.title: FDA says Taylor Farms cyclospora finding was a false positive
stories.description: The retraction comes as over 1,600 people have fallen ill across five states, with the outbreak investigation still active.

stories.title: Spain wins 2026 World Cup with extra-time goal over Argentina
stories.description: Ferran Torres scored in the 106th minute to give Spain a 1-0 victory over Argentina at MetLife Stadium in the final.

stories.title: Trump pressed Carney on wildfire smoke at World Cup final
stories.description: Speaking after the match in New Jersey, Trump said Canada should pay "damages" for smoke drifting into the U.S. from hundreds of wildfires.

stories.title: Homan says ICE body cameras purchased, training underway for nationwide rollout
stories.description: The border czar's Sunday remarks follow two fatal ICE shootings of immigrants during vehicle stops in Houston and Maine this month.

stories.title: Troy Jackson locks up Maine Democratic Senate nod
stories.description: Secretary of State Shenna Bellows dropped out Sunday as Jackson won nearly two-thirds of delegates ahead of next week's nominating convention.

stories.title: FBI told to stop investigating ICE confrontations, NYT reports
stories.description: The reported shift would limit independent scrutiny of immigration agents as nearly half of federal assault cases against protesters have collapsed.

stories.title: Tate brothers arrested in Miami on UK extradition request
stories.description: British prosecutors authorized 38 new charges, bringing the total to 59 across both brothers, including rape, trafficking and indecent images of a child.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 5,100 as IMF funds flow to rebuilding
stories.description: The twin 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes on June 24 have left nearly 20,000 homeless, with the country securing $346 million from the IMF for reconstruction.

stories.title: Trump tentatively agrees to let Saudi Arabia enrich uranium without standard safeguards
stories.description: The draft nuclear accord, awaiting Trump's signature since October 2025, bypasses international inspections designed to prevent weapons development.

stories.title: Iran's reclusive new leader may speak with Putin first, TASS reports
stories.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since succeeding his father in March and will stay hidden for security reasons, an Iranian source said.

stories.title: Israel advances 1,024 new settlement units in West Bank
stories.description: The Palestinian Colonization and Wall Resistance Commission said nine settlement plans have been advanced since early July, targeting over 1,069 dunams of land.

stories.title: Trump finalizes rollback of Endangered Species Act protections
stories.description: The changes eliminate automatic safeguards for threatened species and let regulators weigh economic costs when designating critical habitat.

stories.title: Iran suspends interim deal with US as both sides trade infrastructure strikes
stories.description: Tehran formally abandoned the Islamabad MoU after a seventh straight night of US strikes, with a senior Iranian military adviser warning of "full-scale offensive operations."

stories.title: Iraq, Syria sign US-backed deal to revive oil pipeline bypassing Hormuz
stories.description: The $60 billion package of agreements includes rehabilitating the Kirkuk-Baniyas corridor to carry 2 million barrels per day to the Mediterranean.

stories.title: AIPAC pulls fundraising support for Democrats who voted to cut Israel aid
stories.description: The lobbying group removed donation links for endorsed House Democrats after 103 backed an amendment to eliminate $3.3 billion in military assistance.

stories.title: Khamenei calls Trump's signature 'worthless' as US-Iran MoU collapses
stories.description: Iran's supreme leader warned of "unforgettable lessons" as the U.S. announced its first troop deaths from direct Iranian fire and a seventh night of strikes.

stories.title: Israel destroys 3 more schools in southern Lebanon
stories.description: Lebanon's education minister says Israeli forces looted and demolished three schools, bringing the total destroyed to 20.

stories.title: DHS chief threatens prison for election officials who defy voter roll orders
stories.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin warned of fines and prison time for state officials who refuse to cooperate with federal election security mandates.

stories.title: Protests erupt as Trump's envoy sails into Venice on $450M superyacht
stories.description: Hundreds demonstrated against U.S. Ambassador Tilman Fertitta's arrival, with minor scuffles breaking out as police blocked marchers near the vessel.

stories.title: Apple in early settlement talks with DOJ over antitrust case
stories.description: The discussions, first reported by Bloomberg, come after Apple made multiple settlement offers this year in the smartphone monopoly lawsuit.

stories.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits Mexico's Chiapas coast, triggering tsunami warning
stories.description: The shallow quake struck near Puerto Madero on Friday, shaking buildings as far as Guatemala City and El Salvador.

stories.title: Trump Media pitches $100,000 monthly fee for Truth Social data feed
stories.description: The "Truth API," launching Aug. 1, would give trading firms millisecond-fast access to posts from the platform's most influential accounts.

stories.title: Semiconductor index enters bear market amid global chip sell-off
stories.description: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index has fallen 22% from its June peak, with memory and AI chip stocks plunging across Wall Street and Asia.

stories.title: Andy Burnham becomes Labour leader, set to be PM
stories.description: Burnham ran unopposed after securing support from 379 of 403 Labour MPs, following Keir Starmer's resignation last month.

stories.title: Trump's Gaza Board of Peace abandons sweeping rebuild for small pilot
stories.description: The Board of Peace dropped its $17 billion reconstruction vision in favor of portable cabins near Rafah for tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians.

stories.title: Fired DOJ attorney accuses Blanche of lying at AG hearing
stories.description: Elizabeth Oyer told the Senate Judiciary Committee that Todd Blanche's testimony about her firing over the Mel Gibson gun-rights case was "provably false."

stories.title: SpaceX scrubs Starship Flight 13 after engine ignition failure
stories.description: The abort at Starbase in Texas marks the second consecutive Starship launch scrub, with SpaceX shares falling in after-hours trading.

stories.title: Trump alleges Chinese election meddling in primetime push for stalled voting bill
stories.description: The president declassified intelligence claiming Beijing compromised voter data in 18 states, but U.S. agencies have found no evidence votes were altered.

stories.title: NTSB links deadly 2025 NYC helicopter crash to bird strike
stories.description: Goose remains found on the wreckage offer the first concrete clue to what caused the Hudson River crash that killed six people.

stories.title: Paxton agrees to debate Talarico in tight Texas Senate race
stories.description: The candidates are tied in polls as Paxton ends a decade-long debate absence after Talarico's televised challenge on Thursday.

stories.title: Epstein survivors call Blanche meeting 'disappointing' amid AG confirmation fight
stories.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche met with Epstein survivors Thursday after a key GOP senator demanded it, but attendees called him "abrasive" and "noncommittal."

stories.title: Wildfire smoke, Texas floods hit millions across the US
stories.description: Air quality alerts span Minnesota to New York City as smoke from Canadian wildfires thickens, while flash flooding prompts 75-plus rescues in Texas.

stories.title: Luggage maker's filing pegs DeepSeek valuation at about $52B
stories.description: A routine disclosure by Shenzhen-listed Korrun revealed its subsidiary's indirect stake in DeepSeek, implying a valuation of roughly 351 billion yuan.

stories.title: Top Treasury tax official ousted over IRS audit warnings
stories.description: Kenneth Kies was forced out after warning the White House it risked violating the federal law barring executive branch influence over IRS audits.

stories.title: UN warns Cuba blackouts deepening humanitarian crisis
stories.description: The UN said repeated nationwide outages are disrupting health services, clean water and economic activity across the island.

stories.title: TSMC profit surges 77% to record as AI demand booms
stories.description: Diluted EPS of NT$27.25 beat Wall Street estimates by a wide margin, marking a fifth straight quarter of record earnings.

stories.title: Reflecting Pool peeling likely due to application flaws, not vandalism
stories.description: A Washington Post analysis found the damage pattern tracks with renovation stages, challenging Trump's repeated claims that vandals caused the deterioration.

stories.title: US imposes 25% tariff on thousands of Brazilian imports
stories.description: The Section 301 tariffs, set to take effect July 22, cap a year-long investigation and months of failed negotiations between Washington and Brasilia.

stories.title: Ex-defense chief Esper warns US can't win Iran war from the air
stories.description: Speaking at the Aspen Security Forum, Esper said continued bombing won't change Tehran's behavior and urged economic pressure instead.

stories.title: Trump's teleprompter operator probed for betting on speeches
stories.description: Gabriel Perez allegedly used insider knowledge of presidential remarks to win over $100,000 on prediction market Kalshi.

stories.title: Cornyn says anti-weaponization fund 'not dead' after Blanche hearing
stories.description: The acting AG acknowledged the settlement remains enforceable, leaving key GOP senators undecided on his confirmation to lead the DOJ.

stories.title: Argentina beat England 2-1 to reach World Cup final
stories.description: Lautaro Martínez's stoppage-time goal sent the defending champions past England and into Sunday's final against Spain.

stories.title: Trump leans toward ground troops in Iran as strikes intensify
stories.description: The president was briefed this week on escalatory options including a ground campaign and bombing the Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Musk donates $5M to Ramaswamy's Ohio governor race
stories.description: The contribution signals a thaw between the former DOGE co-leaders, whose partnership ended in a public split in early 2025.

stories.title: CIA says AI drones give Russian troops only minutes to survive on front lines
stories.description: Director Ratcliffe's disclosure comes as Ukraine expands its drone war to the Black Sea, striking 20 Russian vessels overnight.

stories.title: Pentagon weighs Cuba military options, including 101st Airborne air assault
stories.description: CBS News reports that senior defense officials have examined an Army-led air assault plan as tensions over foreign intelligence sites on the island escalate.

stories.title: Trump DNI pick Clayton won't say Biden won 2020 election
stories.description: At his Senate confirmation hearing Wednesday, Clayton said only that Biden was "certified" as president, sparking clashes with Democrats.

stories.title: SpaceX stock falls below $135 IPO price for first time
stories.description: Shares have dropped roughly 40% from their post-listing peak near $225, just over a month after the largest IPO in U.S. history.

stories.title: Hegseth orders annual testosterone screenings for troops over 30
stories.description: The new program offers hormone therapy to service members with clinically low levels, the latest in a series of fitness-focused military reforms.

stories.title: Pew survey: China viewed more favorably than US worldwide
stories.description: A companion report released Wednesday shows confidence in Xi Jinping now exceeds confidence in Trump in 22 countries surveyed.

stories.title: EU and Ukraine sign drone production deal in Kyiv
stories.description: The partnership combines Ukraine's battlefield expertise with EU industrial capacity, with funding drawn from defense loans totaling roughly €100 billion.

stories.title: Goldman's former top lawyer calls Epstein a 'masterful liar' in House testimony
stories.description: Kathryn Ruemmler testified before the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday about her ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: Blanche tells senators he's 'not a yes man' at AG hearing
stories.description: Trump's acting attorney general faced bipartisan scrutiny over his independence and handling of the Epstein files at his confirmation hearing.

stories.title: Iranian oil tankers divert to Karachi as US reinstates naval blockade
stories.description: Two tankers carrying 1 million barrels of crude changed course Tuesday after American forces resumed blocking all traffic to Iranian ports.

stories.title: China's Q2 GDP growth hits 3.5-year low as credit misses
stories.description: Weak lending data and 4.3% second-quarter growth have intensified pressure on Beijing to ramp up fiscal stimulus measures.

stories.title: Mexico files criminal complaints in U.S. over 17 migrant deaths
stories.description: The government escalated from diplomacy to legal action, submitting filings to U.S. prosecutors over deaths in ICE custody and during enforcement raids.

stories.title: Lithuania says Russia planning attacks on infrastructure
stories.description: President Nauseda cited intelligence reports warning of potential Russian strikes on energy and communications sites across the Baltic region.

stories.title: OpenAI's first device is a screenless smart speaker with cameras, Bloomberg reports
stories.description: The portable home companion, designed with Jony Ive, is expected to be unveiled later this year and released in 2027.

stories.title: Wisconsin panel refers Musk to prosecutors over voter bribery
stories.description: The bipartisan Elections Commission voted 5-1 to find probable cause that Musk broke state law by giving $1 million checks to voters before a 2025 judicial race.

stories.title: Spain beats France 2-0 to reach 2026 World Cup final
stories.description: Goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro sealed a dominant semifinal win in Dallas, sending Spain to their first World Cup final since 2010.

stories.title: Nearly a million borrowers leave SAVE student loan plan
stories.description: About 20% moved into older repayment plans that will also be phased out by 2028, potentially forcing a second wave of transitions.

stories.title: Lindsey Graham's sister sworn in as South Carolina senator
stories.description: Darline Graham Nordone became South Carolina's first female U.S. senator Tuesday, filling the seat left vacant by her brother's sudden death.

stories.title: Trump pays E. Jean Carroll $5.6M in sexual abuse case
stories.description: Court filings show the payment was disbursed Monday from escrow, ending years of legal delays over the 2023 jury verdict.

stories.title: Big Banks crush earnings estimates on trading and dealmaking boom
stories.description: The largest U.S. banks reported sharply higher second-quarter profits Tuesday, driven by surging stock trading and a rebound in investment banking activity.

stories.title: UAE frees 55 Iranian fishermen amid Gulf war tensions
stories.description: The rare diplomatic gesture comes as U.S. strikes on Iran continue and Tehran attacks tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Kagan, Barrett testify before Congress on Supreme Court security threats
stories.description: The justices testified before a House panel Tuesday seeking $228 million for security, citing a 38% expected increase in threats.

stories.title: US inflation falls to 3.5% in June, beating forecasts
stories.description: The largest single-month deceleration since April 2020 was fueled by a roughly 10% drop in gasoline prices after a temporary US-Iran deal.

stories.title: Trump cuts two Utah national monuments by about 90%
stories.description: Bears Ears and Grand Staircase-Escalante lost nearly 3 million acres of federal protection Monday, opening land to potential drilling and mining.

stories.title: Appeals court revives 500+ Tylenol autism lawsuits
stories.description: The Second Circuit overturned a 2023 dismissal, allowing families to proceed with claims that prenatal acetaminophen use raises autism and ADHD risk.

stories.title: Nine European nations form missile defense coalition with Ukraine
stories.description: The alliance, announced Monday in Paris, will develop the Freya interceptor system as a cheaper alternative to the Patriot.

stories.title: Judge voids Trump's IRS settlement, refers lawyers for discipline
stories.description: The ruling ends a $1.776 billion "Anti-Weaponization Fund" deal and an addendum that barred IRS audits of Trump and his family.

stories.title: Dubai plans new Fujairah port to bypass Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The Dubai-based port operator aims to build a container terminal on the Gulf of Oman, reducing reliance on the conflict-disrupted waterway.

stories.title: Macron says Europe ready to defend freedom 'at the cost of blood'
stories.description: The French president delivered the warning in his annual Bastille Day eve address as Coalition of the Willing leaders gathered in Paris.

stories.title: U.S. sanctions Cuba's tourism ministry, 9 other entities
stories.description: Monday's designations target the Ministry of Tourism and the Rapid Response Brigades as part of the Trump administration's expanding pressure campaign under EO 14404.

stories.title: Iran warns US-hosting neighbors could become targets
stories.description: Tehran declared that any country allowing its territory to be used for U.S. strikes on Iran would be considered a legitimate target for retaliation.

stories.title: Fire at Bangkok pub kills at least 27 people
stories.description: Flames engulfed Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao near Soi Ladprao 1 early Monday, trapping patrons inside in one of Thailand's deadliest nightlife disasters.

stories.title: Trump administration launches campaign to isolate the ICC
stories.description: The State Department is pursuing travel bans, visa revocations, and expanded sanctions against the Hague-based tribunal's personnel.

stories.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71 after aortic dissection
stories.description: Preliminary findings show the South Carolina Republican died of aortic dissection hours after returning from a trip to Kyiv, Ukraine.

stories.title: Typhoon Bavi slams eastern China after 2.2 million evacuated in Zhejiang
stories.description: The storm weakened to a severe tropical storm Sunday after making landfall Saturday night, but heavy rain and winds continue across the region.

stories.title: Khanna accuses IDF of 'lying' about West Bank detention
stories.description: The congressman's clash with Israel's military escalated Sunday as the Israeli ambassador addressed the incident on national television.

stories.title: Russia runs spy unit from Tokyo to source war tech, NYT finds
stories.description: A New York Times investigation details how a Russian military intelligence unit operates from a Tokyo high-rise, procuring electronics for weapons used in Ukraine.

stories.title: Israeli police arrest four settlers over attack on CNN crew in West Bank
stories.description: The attack in Sinjil came as journalists covered the anniversary of a Palestinian-American's killing by settlers last year.

stories.title: Sweeping housing bill becomes law without Trump's signature
stories.description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act took effect automatically after Trump refused to sign or veto the bipartisan measure.

stories.title: Iran holds crisis talks in Oman as US demands Hormuz pledge
stories.description: After privately admitting the tanker attacks were "a mistake," Tehran faces U.S. pressure to publicly commit to keeping the strait open.

stories.title: US, LATAM allies warn against efforts to undermine Colombia election results
stories.description: More than a dozen countries expressed "deep concern" about unsubstantiated fraud claims following Colombia's razor-thin presidential runoff.

stories.title: Whistleblowers allege no-bid deals, rushed work at Kennedy Center
stories.description: Sen. Whitehouse expanded his probe Friday after disclosures to Congress detailed contracting irregularities tied to the Trump-backed renovation.

stories.title: Hungary joins EU prosecutor's office, unlocking €16.4B in frozen funds
stories.description: The European Commission approved Hungary as the EPPO's 25th member, ending years of resistance under former PM Viktor Orbán.

stories.title: Iran says UAE 'must be held accountable' after US eases export controls
stories.description: Tehran accused Abu Dhabi of complicity in Operation Epic Fury after a Commerce Department filing credited the UAE's role in the campaign.

stories.title: Trump’s renewed praise of May physical reignites scrutiny of president’s mental health
stories.description: The 80-year-old president posted on Truth Social that he requested another cognitive exam, reigniting debate over his health and fitness.

stories.title: Judge vacates Proud Boys' Jan. 6 convictions at DOJ's request
stories.description: Four members had received sentences ranging from 10 to 18 years, including three convicted of seditious conspiracy.

stories.title: NYC adopts 'click to cancel' rule after federal version was struck down
stories.description: Mayor Mamdani announced the first municipal subscription cancellation rule, set to take effect Oct. 1, with penalties starting at $525.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows revenge as Trump threatens to 'decimate' country
stories.description: Mojtaba Khamenei called for retaliation over his father's killing as Trump said 1,000 missiles are "locked and loaded" in response to an assassination plot.

stories.title: Pentagon extends D.C. National Guard mission through 2029
stories.description: Troop levels hit a one-year high of 5,150 as Democratic-led states and D.C. officials push back against the expanding deployment.

stories.title: Meta pulls Muse Image AI tool after backlash over Instagram photo use
stories.description: The company admitted it "missed the mark" just three days after launching the feature, which let anyone generate AI images from public accounts.

stories.title: New York Times countersues EEOC, alleging political retaliation by Trump
stories.description: The countersuit accuses the agency of targeting the newspaper over its journalism, escalating a landmark legal fight over workplace diversity programs.

stories.title: Satellite images show Iran rebuilding bombed nuclear sites
stories.description: A UN official warned Thursday that the IAEA has lost oversight of nearly all Iranian nuclear facilities as reconstruction activity is detected.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft by ex-employees
stories.description: The lawsuit accuses two former Apple executives and OpenAI of a coordinated scheme to steal confidential hardware designs for OpenAI's AI device efforts.

stories.title: Trump, MBS discuss Iran talks amid lingering rift over Hormuz operation
stories.description: Friday's call included U.S. conditions for resuming Iran negotiations, sent to Tehran via the Saudi crown prince.

stories.title: Iran says US strikes hit perimeter of Bushehr nuclear plant
stories.description: Tehran claims projectiles struck near its only civilian nuclear facility as the three-week-old US-Iran ceasefire collapsed amid escalating exchanges of fire.

stories.title: OpenAI, Google sell AI to blacklisted Chinese firms
stories.description: A Financial Times investigation found that Pentagon-blacklisted Chinese companies access U.S. AI models through Singapore subsidiaries.

stories.title: DHS investigators weigh criminal referral of Lewandowski over contracts
stories.description: Evidence suggests the former top aide to Kristi Noem was involved in improperly awarding government contracts, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Victor Marx wins Colorado GOP primary for governor
stories.description: The Marine veteran and ministry founder will face Attorney General Phil Weiser in the November general election.

stories.title: China, Russia, Iran amplify US backlash to AI data centers
stories.description: State media outlets exploited local concerns over energy costs and environmental impacts to undermine American AI infrastructure, according to new reports.

stories.title: Trump-backed Freedom Fuel stations raise questions over opaque ownership and pricing
stories.description: The 25 discount gas stations across Pennsylvania and New Jersey are already seeing prices climb above their debut $3.47 level.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says China warned Russia against nuclear use
stories.description: The Ukrainian president said Beijing issued an "ultimatum"-style warning to Moscow, marking what he called China's firmest stance since the invasion began.

stories.title: Kirk suspect wept and expressed regret, roommate tells court
stories.description: Lance Twiggs testified that Tyler Robinson confessed to killing Charlie Kirk two days after the shooting, then cried and said he wished he hadn't done it.

stories.title: Pressure grows on Yale to reject Trump admin deal
stories.description: Faculty, students, alumni and Sen. Blumenthal are urging the university to reject any deal over admissions discrimination allegations.

stories.title: Iran races to export oil as US sanctions deadline nears
stories.description: About 63 million barrels of Iranian crude sit stranded on tankers as Tehran scrambles to ship oil before a July 17 wind-down deadline.

stories.title: Meta to begin producing in-house AI chip 'Iris' in September
stories.description: An internal memo shows the company plans to manufacture its "Iris" data center chip as it aims to double computing capacity to 14 gigawatts by 2027.

stories.title: Trump fires all Election Assistance Commission members
stories.description: The dismissals leave the federal election agency without leadership months before the November midterms.

stories.title: Idaho mother who blamed vaccines for twins' deaths charged with murder
stories.description: Andrea Shaw, lead plaintiff in a Children's Health Defense lawsuit, was indicted on two counts of first-degree murder after a grand jury found she suffocated her toddlers.

stories.title: Former judge who helped immigrant evade ICE spared prison, fined $5K
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Lynn Adelman cited Hannah Dugan's law-abiding life, rejecting prosecutors' push for 15 to 21 months behind bars.

stories.title: Poorly worded clause in Trump's Iran deal fuels Hormuz crisis
stories.description: A dispute over Paragraph 5 of the U.S.-Iran agreement has triggered repeated confrontations at the strait, with nearly 6,000 seafarers stranded.

stories.title: Blue Origin raising $10B in first outside funding round
stories.description: The rocket company, funded almost entirely by Jeff Bezos for 26 years, is valued at $130 billion in the deal led by Coatue Management.

stories.title: Former Pfizer building in Manhattan deemed stable after collapse scare
stories.description: Most evacuated residents have been cleared to return as the restricted zone shrinks and an investigation into the structural failure continues.

stories.title: Millions gather as Khamenei funeral continues in Iraq
stories.description: Massive processions filled Najaf and Karbala on Wednesday as Trump declared the U.S.-Iran ceasefire "over" and signaled further military action.

stories.title: Second accuser deepens crisis for Maine Senate nominee Platner
stories.description: A former girlfriend alleged stealthing and physical abuse on CNN Tuesday, as Sanders and Warren joined calls for Platner to exit the race.

stories.title: Le Pen says she will run for France’s presidency in 2027
stories.description: A Paris appeals court upheld her embezzlement conviction but reduced her office ban, making her immediately eligible to run next April.

stories.title: Trump offers Ukraine Patriot license, backs strikes on Russia
stories.description: At the NATO summit in Ankara, Trump endorsed Ukraine's deep strikes on Russian refineries and offered a license to produce Patriot missiles.

stories.title: Ukraine finds Monaco bombing suspect shot dead near Kyiv
stories.description: A military intelligence officer confessed to killing Anastasiia Berezovska, the Interpol-wanted suspect in the June 29 attack on a Ukrainian tycoon.

stories.title: US trade deficit jumps 42% in May on record imports
stories.description: Capital goods imports hit a record $128 billion, driven by semiconductors and computer accessories tied to AI data center buildout.

stories.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to rehear birthright citizenship case
stories.description: The president called the court's 6-3 ruling striking down his executive order "a miscarriage of justice" and demanded immediate reconsideration.

stories.title: Belgium routs USMNT 4-1 to end US World Cup run
stories.description: Charles De Ketelaere scored twice as Belgium eliminated the United States in the Round of 16 on Monday in Seattle.

stories.title: CISA now using Anthropic's Mythos to audit government code
stories.description: The deployment caps months of effort to get the powerful AI vulnerability-detection model into the hands of the lead U.S. civilian cybersecurity agency.

stories.title: SSN glitch delays some parents from opening Trump Accounts for newborns
stories.description: The Treasury Department says a verification issue is blocking some 2026 babies with newly issued Social Security numbers from enrollment.

stories.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, offers power to UN-backed panel
stories.description: Israel dismissed the move as "deception with no practical significance," saying Hamas members remained in their positions.

stories.title: Preliminary hearing begins for man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
stories.description: Prosecutors in Provo are presenting evidence against Tyler Robinson as Kirk's widow and parents face the defendant for the first time.

stories.title: Trump declares Iran ceasefire 'over,' threatens to cut trade with Spain at NATO summit
stories.description: As U.S. and Iranian forces traded strikes overnight, the president told allies in Ankara the preliminary truce was finished and singled out Spain for punishment.

stories.title: Three Khamenei sons appear at funeral; successor stays hidden
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei remains absent from his father's massive state funeral over fears Israel could assassinate him.

stories.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed at Madison Square Garden
stories.description: Adam Sandler officiated as roughly 1,000 guests watched the couple marry Friday night in custom Dior at the iconic NYC arena.

top.title: Pentagon confirms nearly 100 troops injured in Iran war since July 7
top.description: The death toll has risen to 17 since Operation Epic Fury began in February, with three service members killed over the weekend.

top.title: Israel says Iran moved centrifuges into Pickaxe Mountain
top.description: The intelligence was shared with the U.S. as Trump weighs strikes on the fortified underground nuclear site near Natanz.

top.title: Congo Ebola death toll hits 930 as outbreak accelerates
top.description: WHO calls it the third-largest Ebola outbreak on record, now expanding faster than any previous one.

top.title: Ex-soldier arrested after fire outside NYC Federal Plaza
top.description: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, allegedly poured gasoline and ignited fireworks outside 26 Federal Plaza in an anti-ICE attack that injured three people.

top.title: New UK PM Burnham speaks with Trump on defense, Hormuz
top.description: Hours after taking office Monday, Burnham congratulated Trump on the World Cup and pledged UK commitment to securing the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Judge freezes Paramount's $111B Warner Bros. merger
top.description: A federal judge granted 12 states a temporary restraining order blocking the deal, which Paramount had planned to close this week.

top.title: Pentagon denies hiding troop injuries after undisclosed Iran strikes revealed
top.description: The Defense Department pushed back on a New York Times report that three earlier Iranian strikes on U.S. forces in Jordan went unreported.

top.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian autos, dairy, alcohol
top.description: The duties, invoked under Section 338, take effect in 30 days and would apply even to goods previously protected under USMCA.

top.title: Ortega declares Nicaragua will never hold elections again
top.description: The 80-year-old president made the announcement during a speech marking the 47th anniversary of the Sandinista revolution on Sunday.

top.title: Pakistan, Qatar urge US and Iran to accept two-week ceasefire
top.description: The joint proposal calls for a return to pre-July 9 positions, reopening the Strait of Hormuz, and lifting the US blockade to restart stalled talks.

top.title: Sen. Darline Graham launches bid for full Senate term
top.description: Graham told Fox News' Sean Hannity she's "in" the Aug. 11 special GOP primary, backed by Trump's endorsement, to succeed her late brother Lindsey.

top.title: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder El Mayo Zambada sentenced to life in U.S. prison
top.description: The 76-year-old drug kingpin also faces $15 billion in financial penalties, though experts say recovering more than a fraction is unlikely.

top.title: Houthis declare naval blockade against Saudi Arabia
top.description: The militant group announced an immediate maritime embargo, framing it as retaliation for nearly 12 years of Saudi siege on Yemen's ports.

top.title: Qatar-gifted Air Force One to get security upgrades this fall, White House says
top.description: Trump will revert to the older presidential aircraft while the Qatari-donated jet undergoes about a month of enhancements to its defensive capabilities.

top.title: Hamas elects chief negotiator al-Hayya as new leader
top.description: The group's top ceasefire negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in October 2024, after a months-long internal vote.

top.title: Rubio says US open to diplomacy after ninth night of Iran strikes
top.description: The secretary of state confirmed three service member deaths and called on allies to help defend the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: DOJ sought phone records of NYT journalists' relatives
top.description: The Trump administration subpoenaed phone records of reporters' family members, including one journalist's mother, in its Air Force One leak probe.

top.title: Ukraine fires 400 drones at Moscow region overnight
top.description: The massive drone barrage followed Russia's record ballistic missile attack on Kyiv the previous night, which killed at least one person and wounded 16.

top.title: Andy Burnham takes office as UK prime minister
top.description: The former Greater Manchester mayor succeeds Keir Starmer, pledging to "rewire" Britain with a focus on devolution and cost-of-living relief.

top.title: Trump vows Netanyahu won't be arrested in the U.S.
top.description: The president responded on Truth Social to NYC Mayor Mamdani's push to detain the Israeli prime minister during September's UN General Assembly.

top.title: FDA says Taylor Farms cyclospora finding was a false positive
top.description: The retraction comes as over 1,600 people have fallen ill across five states, with the outbreak investigation still active.

top.title: Spain wins 2026 World Cup with extra-time goal over Argentina
top.description: Ferran Torres scored in the 106th minute to give Spain a 1-0 victory over Argentina at MetLife Stadium in the final.

top.title: Trump pressed Carney on wildfire smoke at World Cup final
top.description: Speaking after the match in New Jersey, Trump said Canada should pay "damages" for smoke drifting into the U.S. from hundreds of wildfires.

top.title: Homan says ICE body cameras purchased, training underway for nationwide rollout
top.description: The border czar's Sunday remarks follow two fatal ICE shootings of immigrants during vehicle stops in Houston and Maine this month.

top.title: Troy Jackson locks up Maine Democratic Senate nod
top.description: Secretary of State Shenna Bellows dropped out Sunday as Jackson won nearly two-thirds of delegates ahead of next week's nominating convention.

top.title: FBI told to stop investigating ICE confrontations, NYT reports
top.description: The reported shift would limit independent scrutiny of immigration agents as nearly half of federal assault cases against protesters have collapsed.

top.title: Tate brothers arrested in Miami on UK extradition request
top.description: British prosecutors authorized 38 new charges, bringing the total to 59 across both brothers, including rape, trafficking and indecent images of a child.

top.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 5,100 as IMF funds flow to rebuilding
top.description: The twin 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes on June 24 have left nearly 20,000 homeless, with the country securing $346 million from the IMF for reconstruction.

top.title: Trump tentatively agrees to let Saudi Arabia enrich uranium without standard safeguards
top.description: The draft nuclear accord, awaiting Trump's signature since October 2025, bypasses international inspections designed to prevent weapons development.

top.title: Iran's reclusive new leader may speak with Putin first, TASS reports
top.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since succeeding his father in March and will stay hidden for security reasons, an Iranian source said.

top.title: Israel advances 1,024 new settlement units in West Bank
top.description: The Palestinian Colonization and Wall Resistance Commission said nine settlement plans have been advanced since early July, targeting over 1,069 dunams of land.

top.title: Trump finalizes rollback of Endangered Species Act protections
top.description: The changes eliminate automatic safeguards for threatened species and let regulators weigh economic costs when designating critical habitat.

top.title: Iran suspends interim deal with US as both sides trade infrastructure strikes
top.description: Tehran formally abandoned the Islamabad MoU after a seventh straight night of US strikes, with a senior Iranian military adviser warning of "full-scale offensive operations."

top.title: Iraq, Syria sign US-backed deal to revive oil pipeline bypassing Hormuz
top.description: The $60 billion package of agreements includes rehabilitating the Kirkuk-Baniyas corridor to carry 2 million barrels per day to the Mediterranean.

top.title: AIPAC pulls fundraising support for Democrats who voted to cut Israel aid
top.description: The lobbying group removed donation links for endorsed House Democrats after 103 backed an amendment to eliminate $3.3 billion in military assistance.

top.title: Khamenei calls Trump's signature 'worthless' as US-Iran MoU collapses
top.description: Iran's supreme leader warned of "unforgettable lessons" as the U.S. announced its first troop deaths from direct Iranian fire and a seventh night of strikes.

top.title: Israel destroys 3 more schools in southern Lebanon
top.description: Lebanon's education minister says Israeli forces looted and demolished three schools, bringing the total destroyed to 20.

top.title: DHS chief threatens prison for election officials who defy voter roll orders
top.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin warned of fines and prison time for state officials who refuse to cooperate with federal election security mandates.

top.title: Protests erupt as Trump's envoy sails into Venice on $450M superyacht
top.description: Hundreds demonstrated against U.S. Ambassador Tilman Fertitta's arrival, with minor scuffles breaking out as police blocked marchers near the vessel.

top.title: Apple in early settlement talks with DOJ over antitrust case
top.description: The discussions, first reported by Bloomberg, come after Apple made multiple settlement offers this year in the smartphone monopoly lawsuit.

top.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits Mexico's Chiapas coast, triggering tsunami warning
top.description: The shallow quake struck near Puerto Madero on Friday, shaking buildings as far as Guatemala City and El Salvador.

top.title: Trump Media pitches $100,000 monthly fee for Truth Social data feed
top.description: The "Truth API," launching Aug. 1, would give trading firms millisecond-fast access to posts from the platform's most influential accounts.

top.title: Semiconductor index enters bear market amid global chip sell-off
top.description: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index has fallen 22% from its June peak, with memory and AI chip stocks plunging across Wall Street and Asia.

top.title: Andy Burnham becomes Labour leader, set to be PM
top.description: Burnham ran unopposed after securing support from 379 of 403 Labour MPs, following Keir Starmer's resignation last month.

top.title: Trump's Gaza Board of Peace abandons sweeping rebuild for small pilot
top.description: The Board of Peace dropped its $17 billion reconstruction vision in favor of portable cabins near Rafah for tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians.

top.title: Fired DOJ attorney accuses Blanche of lying at AG hearing
top.description: Elizabeth Oyer told the Senate Judiciary Committee that Todd Blanche's testimony about her firing over the Mel Gibson gun-rights case was "provably false."

top.title: SpaceX scrubs Starship Flight 13 after engine ignition failure
top.description: The abort at Starbase in Texas marks the second consecutive Starship launch scrub, with SpaceX shares falling in after-hours trading.

top.title: Trump alleges Chinese election meddling in primetime push for stalled voting bill
top.description: The president declassified intelligence claiming Beijing compromised voter data in 18 states, but U.S. agencies have found no evidence votes were altered.

top.title: NTSB links deadly 2025 NYC helicopter crash to bird strike
top.description: Goose remains found on the wreckage offer the first concrete clue to what caused the Hudson River crash that killed six people.

top.title: Paxton agrees to debate Talarico in tight Texas Senate race
top.description: The candidates are tied in polls as Paxton ends a decade-long debate absence after Talarico's televised challenge on Thursday.

top.title: Epstein survivors call Blanche meeting 'disappointing' amid AG confirmation fight
top.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche met with Epstein survivors Thursday after a key GOP senator demanded it, but attendees called him "abrasive" and "noncommittal."

top.title: Wildfire smoke, Texas floods hit millions across the US
top.description: Air quality alerts span Minnesota to New York City as smoke from Canadian wildfires thickens, while flash flooding prompts 75-plus rescues in Texas.

top.title: Luggage maker's filing pegs DeepSeek valuation at about $52B
top.description: A routine disclosure by Shenzhen-listed Korrun revealed its subsidiary's indirect stake in DeepSeek, implying a valuation of roughly 351 billion yuan.

top.title: Top Treasury tax official ousted over IRS audit warnings
top.description: Kenneth Kies was forced out after warning the White House it risked violating the federal law barring executive branch influence over IRS audits.

top.title: UN warns Cuba blackouts deepening humanitarian crisis
top.description: The UN said repeated nationwide outages are disrupting health services, clean water and economic activity across the island.

top.title: TSMC profit surges 77% to record as AI demand booms
top.description: Diluted EPS of NT$27.25 beat Wall Street estimates by a wide margin, marking a fifth straight quarter of record earnings.

top.title: Reflecting Pool peeling likely due to application flaws, not vandalism
top.description: A Washington Post analysis found the damage pattern tracks with renovation stages, challenging Trump's repeated claims that vandals caused the deterioration.

top.title: US imposes 25% tariff on thousands of Brazilian imports
top.description: The Section 301 tariffs, set to take effect July 22, cap a year-long investigation and months of failed negotiations between Washington and Brasilia.

top.title: Ex-defense chief Esper warns US can't win Iran war from the air
top.description: Speaking at the Aspen Security Forum, Esper said continued bombing won't change Tehran's behavior and urged economic pressure instead.

top.title: Trump's teleprompter operator probed for betting on speeches
top.description: Gabriel Perez allegedly used insider knowledge of presidential remarks to win over $100,000 on prediction market Kalshi.

top.title: Cornyn says anti-weaponization fund 'not dead' after Blanche hearing
top.description: The acting AG acknowledged the settlement remains enforceable, leaving key GOP senators undecided on his confirmation to lead the DOJ.

top.title: Argentina beat England 2-1 to reach World Cup final
top.description: Lautaro Martínez's stoppage-time goal sent the defending champions past England and into Sunday's final against Spain.

top.title: Trump leans toward ground troops in Iran as strikes intensify
top.description: The president was briefed this week on escalatory options including a ground campaign and bombing the Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Musk donates $5M to Ramaswamy's Ohio governor race
top.description: The contribution signals a thaw between the former DOGE co-leaders, whose partnership ended in a public split in early 2025.

top.title: CIA says AI drones give Russian troops only minutes to survive on front lines
top.description: Director Ratcliffe's disclosure comes as Ukraine expands its drone war to the Black Sea, striking 20 Russian vessels overnight.

top.title: Pentagon weighs Cuba military options, including 101st Airborne air assault
top.description: CBS News reports that senior defense officials have examined an Army-led air assault plan as tensions over foreign intelligence sites on the island escalate.

top.title: Trump DNI pick Clayton won't say Biden won 2020 election
top.description: At his Senate confirmation hearing Wednesday, Clayton said only that Biden was "certified" as president, sparking clashes with Democrats.

top.title: SpaceX stock falls below $135 IPO price for first time
top.description: Shares have dropped roughly 40% from their post-listing peak near $225, just over a month after the largest IPO in U.S. history.

top.title: Hegseth orders annual testosterone screenings for troops over 30
top.description: The new program offers hormone therapy to service members with clinically low levels, the latest in a series of fitness-focused military reforms.

top.title: Pew survey: China viewed more favorably than US worldwide
top.description: A companion report released Wednesday shows confidence in Xi Jinping now exceeds confidence in Trump in 22 countries surveyed.

top.title: EU and Ukraine sign drone production deal in Kyiv
top.description: The partnership combines Ukraine's battlefield expertise with EU industrial capacity, with funding drawn from defense loans totaling roughly €100 billion.

top.title: Goldman's former top lawyer calls Epstein a 'masterful liar' in House testimony
top.description: Kathryn Ruemmler testified before the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday about her ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

top.title: Blanche tells senators he's 'not a yes man' at AG hearing
top.description: Trump's acting attorney general faced bipartisan scrutiny over his independence and handling of the Epstein files at his confirmation hearing.

top.title: Iranian oil tankers divert to Karachi as US reinstates naval blockade
top.description: Two tankers carrying 1 million barrels of crude changed course Tuesday after American forces resumed blocking all traffic to Iranian ports.

top.title: China's Q2 GDP growth hits 3.5-year low as credit misses
top.description: Weak lending data and 4.3% second-quarter growth have intensified pressure on Beijing to ramp up fiscal stimulus measures.

top.title: Mexico files criminal complaints in U.S. over 17 migrant deaths
top.description: The government escalated from diplomacy to legal action, submitting filings to U.S. prosecutors over deaths in ICE custody and during enforcement raids.

top.title: Lithuania says Russia planning attacks on infrastructure
top.description: President Nauseda cited intelligence reports warning of potential Russian strikes on energy and communications sites across the Baltic region.

top.title: OpenAI's first device is a screenless smart speaker with cameras, Bloomberg reports
top.description: The portable home companion, designed with Jony Ive, is expected to be unveiled later this year and released in 2027.

top.title: Wisconsin panel refers Musk to prosecutors over voter bribery
top.description: The bipartisan Elections Commission voted 5-1 to find probable cause that Musk broke state law by giving $1 million checks to voters before a 2025 judicial race.

top.title: Spain beats France 2-0 to reach 2026 World Cup final
top.description: Goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro sealed a dominant semifinal win in Dallas, sending Spain to their first World Cup final since 2010.

top.title: Nearly a million borrowers leave SAVE student loan plan
top.description: About 20% moved into older repayment plans that will also be phased out by 2028, potentially forcing a second wave of transitions.

top.title: Lindsey Graham's sister sworn in as South Carolina senator
top.description: Darline Graham Nordone became South Carolina's first female U.S. senator Tuesday, filling the seat left vacant by her brother's sudden death.

top.title: Trump pays E. Jean Carroll $5.6M in sexual abuse case
top.description: Court filings show the payment was disbursed Monday from escrow, ending years of legal delays over the 2023 jury verdict.

top.title: Big Banks crush earnings estimates on trading and dealmaking boom
top.description: The largest U.S. banks reported sharply higher second-quarter profits Tuesday, driven by surging stock trading and a rebound in investment banking activity.

top.title: UAE frees 55 Iranian fishermen amid Gulf war tensions
top.description: The rare diplomatic gesture comes as U.S. strikes on Iran continue and Tehran attacks tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Kagan, Barrett testify before Congress on Supreme Court security threats
top.description: The justices testified before a House panel Tuesday seeking $228 million for security, citing a 38% expected increase in threats.

top.title: US inflation falls to 3.5% in June, beating forecasts
top.description: The largest single-month deceleration since April 2020 was fueled by a roughly 10% drop in gasoline prices after a temporary US-Iran deal.

top.title: Trump cuts two Utah national monuments by about 90%
top.description: Bears Ears and Grand Staircase-Escalante lost nearly 3 million acres of federal protection Monday, opening land to potential drilling and mining.

top.title: Appeals court revives 500+ Tylenol autism lawsuits
top.description: The Second Circuit overturned a 2023 dismissal, allowing families to proceed with claims that prenatal acetaminophen use raises autism and ADHD risk.

top.title: Nine European nations form missile defense coalition with Ukraine
top.description: The alliance, announced Monday in Paris, will develop the Freya interceptor system as a cheaper alternative to the Patriot.

top.title: Judge voids Trump's IRS settlement, refers lawyers for discipline
top.description: The ruling ends a $1.776 billion "Anti-Weaponization Fund" deal and an addendum that barred IRS audits of Trump and his family.

top.title: Dubai plans new Fujairah port to bypass Strait of Hormuz
top.description: The Dubai-based port operator aims to build a container terminal on the Gulf of Oman, reducing reliance on the conflict-disrupted waterway.

top.title: Macron says Europe ready to defend freedom 'at the cost of blood'
top.description: The French president delivered the warning in his annual Bastille Day eve address as Coalition of the Willing leaders gathered in Paris.

top.title: U.S. sanctions Cuba's tourism ministry, 9 other entities
top.description: Monday's designations target the Ministry of Tourism and the Rapid Response Brigades as part of the Trump administration's expanding pressure campaign under EO 14404.

top.title: Iran warns US-hosting neighbors could become targets
top.description: Tehran declared that any country allowing its territory to be used for U.S. strikes on Iran would be considered a legitimate target for retaliation.

top.title: Fire at Bangkok pub kills at least 27 people
top.description: Flames engulfed Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao near Soi Ladprao 1 early Monday, trapping patrons inside in one of Thailand's deadliest nightlife disasters.

top.title: Trump administration launches campaign to isolate the ICC
top.description: The State Department is pursuing travel bans, visa revocations, and expanded sanctions against the Hague-based tribunal's personnel.

top.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71 after aortic dissection
top.description: Preliminary findings show the South Carolina Republican died of aortic dissection hours after returning from a trip to Kyiv, Ukraine.

top.title: Typhoon Bavi slams eastern China after 2.2 million evacuated in Zhejiang
top.description: The storm weakened to a severe tropical storm Sunday after making landfall Saturday night, but heavy rain and winds continue across the region.

top.title: Khanna accuses IDF of 'lying' about West Bank detention
top.description: The congressman's clash with Israel's military escalated Sunday as the Israeli ambassador addressed the incident on national television.

top.title: Russia runs spy unit from Tokyo to source war tech, NYT finds
top.description: A New York Times investigation details how a Russian military intelligence unit operates from a Tokyo high-rise, procuring electronics for weapons used in Ukraine.

top.title: Israeli police arrest four settlers over attack on CNN crew in West Bank
top.description: The attack in Sinjil came as journalists covered the anniversary of a Palestinian-American's killing by settlers last year.

top.title: Sweeping housing bill becomes law without Trump's signature
top.description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act took effect automatically after Trump refused to sign or veto the bipartisan measure.

top.title: Iran holds crisis talks in Oman as US demands Hormuz pledge
top.description: After privately admitting the tanker attacks were "a mistake," Tehran faces U.S. pressure to publicly commit to keeping the strait open.

top.title: US, LATAM allies warn against efforts to undermine Colombia election results
top.description: More than a dozen countries expressed "deep concern" about unsubstantiated fraud claims following Colombia's razor-thin presidential runoff.

top.title: Whistleblowers allege no-bid deals, rushed work at Kennedy Center
top.description: Sen. Whitehouse expanded his probe Friday after disclosures to Congress detailed contracting irregularities tied to the Trump-backed renovation.

top.title: Hungary joins EU prosecutor's office, unlocking €16.4B in frozen funds
top.description: The European Commission approved Hungary as the EPPO's 25th member, ending years of resistance under former PM Viktor Orbán.

top.title: Iran says UAE 'must be held accountable' after US eases export controls
top.description: Tehran accused Abu Dhabi of complicity in Operation Epic Fury after a Commerce Department filing credited the UAE's role in the campaign.

top.title: Trump’s renewed praise of May physical reignites scrutiny of president’s mental health
top.description: The 80-year-old president posted on Truth Social that he requested another cognitive exam, reigniting debate over his health and fitness.

top.title: Judge vacates Proud Boys' Jan. 6 convictions at DOJ's request
top.description: Four members had received sentences ranging from 10 to 18 years, including three convicted of seditious conspiracy.

top.title: NYC adopts 'click to cancel' rule after federal version was struck down
top.description: Mayor Mamdani announced the first municipal subscription cancellation rule, set to take effect Oct. 1, with penalties starting at $525.

top.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows revenge as Trump threatens to 'decimate' country
top.description: Mojtaba Khamenei called for retaliation over his father's killing as Trump said 1,000 missiles are "locked and loaded" in response to an assassination plot.

top.title: Pentagon extends D.C. National Guard mission through 2029
top.description: Troop levels hit a one-year high of 5,150 as Democratic-led states and D.C. officials push back against the expanding deployment.

top.title: Meta pulls Muse Image AI tool after backlash over Instagram photo use
top.description: The company admitted it "missed the mark" just three days after launching the feature, which let anyone generate AI images from public accounts.

top.title: New York Times countersues EEOC, alleging political retaliation by Trump
top.description: The countersuit accuses the agency of targeting the newspaper over its journalism, escalating a landmark legal fight over workplace diversity programs.

top.title: Satellite images show Iran rebuilding bombed nuclear sites
top.description: A UN official warned Thursday that the IAEA has lost oversight of nearly all Iranian nuclear facilities as reconstruction activity is detected.

top.title: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft by ex-employees
top.description: The lawsuit accuses two former Apple executives and OpenAI of a coordinated scheme to steal confidential hardware designs for OpenAI's AI device efforts.

top.title: Trump, MBS discuss Iran talks amid lingering rift over Hormuz operation
top.description: Friday's call included U.S. conditions for resuming Iran negotiations, sent to Tehran via the Saudi crown prince.

top.title: Iran says US strikes hit perimeter of Bushehr nuclear plant
top.description: Tehran claims projectiles struck near its only civilian nuclear facility as the three-week-old US-Iran ceasefire collapsed amid escalating exchanges of fire.

top.title: OpenAI, Google sell AI to blacklisted Chinese firms
top.description: A Financial Times investigation found that Pentagon-blacklisted Chinese companies access U.S. AI models through Singapore subsidiaries.

top.title: DHS investigators weigh criminal referral of Lewandowski over contracts
top.description: Evidence suggests the former top aide to Kristi Noem was involved in improperly awarding government contracts, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Victor Marx wins Colorado GOP primary for governor
top.description: The Marine veteran and ministry founder will face Attorney General Phil Weiser in the November general election.

top.title: China, Russia, Iran amplify US backlash to AI data centers
top.description: State media outlets exploited local concerns over energy costs and environmental impacts to undermine American AI infrastructure, according to new reports.

top.title: Trump-backed Freedom Fuel stations raise questions over opaque ownership and pricing
top.description: The 25 discount gas stations across Pennsylvania and New Jersey are already seeing prices climb above their debut $3.47 level.

top.title: Zelenskyy says China warned Russia against nuclear use
top.description: The Ukrainian president said Beijing issued an "ultimatum"-style warning to Moscow, marking what he called China's firmest stance since the invasion began.

top.title: Kirk suspect wept and expressed regret, roommate tells court
top.description: Lance Twiggs testified that Tyler Robinson confessed to killing Charlie Kirk two days after the shooting, then cried and said he wished he hadn't done it.

top.title: Pressure grows on Yale to reject Trump admin deal
top.description: Faculty, students, alumni and Sen. Blumenthal are urging the university to reject any deal over admissions discrimination allegations.

top.title: Iran races to export oil as US sanctions deadline nears
top.description: About 63 million barrels of Iranian crude sit stranded on tankers as Tehran scrambles to ship oil before a July 17 wind-down deadline.

top.title: Meta to begin producing in-house AI chip 'Iris' in September
top.description: An internal memo shows the company plans to manufacture its "Iris" data center chip as it aims to double computing capacity to 14 gigawatts by 2027.

top.title: Trump fires all Election Assistance Commission members
top.description: The dismissals leave the federal election agency without leadership months before the November midterms.

top.title: Idaho mother who blamed vaccines for twins' deaths charged with murder
top.description: Andrea Shaw, lead plaintiff in a Children's Health Defense lawsuit, was indicted on two counts of first-degree murder after a grand jury found she suffocated her toddlers.

top.title: Former judge who helped immigrant evade ICE spared prison, fined $5K
top.description: U.S. District Judge Lynn Adelman cited Hannah Dugan's law-abiding life, rejecting prosecutors' push for 15 to 21 months behind bars.

top.title: Poorly worded clause in Trump's Iran deal fuels Hormuz crisis
top.description: A dispute over Paragraph 5 of the U.S.-Iran agreement has triggered repeated confrontations at the strait, with nearly 6,000 seafarers stranded.

top.title: Blue Origin raising $10B in first outside funding round
top.description: The rocket company, funded almost entirely by Jeff Bezos for 26 years, is valued at $130 billion in the deal led by Coatue Management.

top.title: Former Pfizer building in Manhattan deemed stable after collapse scare
top.description: Most evacuated residents have been cleared to return as the restricted zone shrinks and an investigation into the structural failure continues.

top.title: Millions gather as Khamenei funeral continues in Iraq
top.description: Massive processions filled Najaf and Karbala on Wednesday as Trump declared the U.S.-Iran ceasefire "over" and signaled further military action.

top.title: Second accuser deepens crisis for Maine Senate nominee Platner
top.description: A former girlfriend alleged stealthing and physical abuse on CNN Tuesday, as Sanders and Warren joined calls for Platner to exit the race.

top.title: Le Pen says she will run for France’s presidency in 2027
top.description: A Paris appeals court upheld her embezzlement conviction but reduced her office ban, making her immediately eligible to run next April.

top.title: Trump offers Ukraine Patriot license, backs strikes on Russia
top.description: At the NATO summit in Ankara, Trump endorsed Ukraine's deep strikes on Russian refineries and offered a license to produce Patriot missiles.

top.title: Ukraine finds Monaco bombing suspect shot dead near Kyiv
top.description: A military intelligence officer confessed to killing Anastasiia Berezovska, the Interpol-wanted suspect in the June 29 attack on a Ukrainian tycoon.

top.title: US trade deficit jumps 42% in May on record imports
top.description: Capital goods imports hit a record $128 billion, driven by semiconductors and computer accessories tied to AI data center buildout.

top.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to rehear birthright citizenship case
top.description: The president called the court's 6-3 ruling striking down his executive order "a miscarriage of justice" and demanded immediate reconsideration.

top.title: Belgium routs USMNT 4-1 to end US World Cup run
top.description: Charles De Ketelaere scored twice as Belgium eliminated the United States in the Round of 16 on Monday in Seattle.

top.title: CISA now using Anthropic's Mythos to audit government code
top.description: The deployment caps months of effort to get the powerful AI vulnerability-detection model into the hands of the lead U.S. civilian cybersecurity agency.

top.title: SSN glitch delays some parents from opening Trump Accounts for newborns
top.description: The Treasury Department says a verification issue is blocking some 2026 babies with newly issued Social Security numbers from enrollment.

top.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, offers power to UN-backed panel
top.description: Israel dismissed the move as "deception with no practical significance," saying Hamas members remained in their positions.

top.title: Preliminary hearing begins for man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
top.description: Prosecutors in Provo are presenting evidence against Tyler Robinson as Kirk's widow and parents face the defendant for the first time.

top.title: Trump declares Iran ceasefire 'over,' threatens to cut trade with Spain at NATO summit
top.description: As U.S. and Iranian forces traded strikes overnight, the president told allies in Ankara the preliminary truce was finished and singled out Spain for punishment.

top.title: Three Khamenei sons appear at funeral; successor stays hidden
top.description: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei remains absent from his father's massive state funeral over fears Israel could assassinate him.

top.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed at Madison Square Garden
top.description: Adam Sandler officiated as roughly 1,000 guests watched the couple marry Friday night in custom Dior at the iconic NYC arena.
